litigation.type: Federal statutory claims (Camp Lejeune Justice Act of 2022)
litigation.mdlName: Camp Lejeune Justice Act litigation (not an MDL)
litigation.court: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of North Carolina (exclusive jurisdiction)
litigation.judge: Cases distributed among the district's judges; magistrate coordination

phase: Settlement processing and first trials — filing window closed
phaseDetail: The two-year CLJA filing window closed on August 10, 2024. Pending administrative claims and filed lawsuits continue to move through the DOJ/Navy Elective Option settlement program and Track 1 bellwether trial preparation in the Eastern District of North Carolina.

pendingCounts.scope: Administrative claims filed with the Navy (approximate)

defendants: United States of America (Department of the Navy)

settlements.label: Elective Option settlement program
settlements.detail: The DOJ/Navy Elective Option offers tiered payments of roughly $100,000 to $450,000 depending on diagnosis and exposure duration, plus $100,000 for qualifying wrongful-death claims (up to $550,000 combined). Per DOJ figures dated May 15, 2026, more than $876 million in settlements had been offered and approximately $665 million paid. Elective Option payments are not reduced by VA benefit offsets; recoveries outside the program may be subject to the CLJA's offset provisions.

keyDates.label: Camp Lejeune Justice Act signed
keyDates.detail: CLJA created a federal cause of action for those exposed to contaminated water at Camp Lejeune for at least 30 days between August 1, 1953 and December 31, 1987.

keyDates.label: Elective Option announced
keyDates.detail: DOJ and Navy launched a tiered early-settlement program for qualifying diagnoses.

keyDates.label: Filing deadline passed
keyDates.detail: The CLJA two-year window to file administrative claims closed. New claims are generally barred.

keyDates.label: Settlements pass $876M offered / $665M paid
keyDates.detail: DOJ reported more than $876 million offered and approximately $665 million paid as of May 15, 2026, with offers continuing weekly.

keyDates.label: Track 1 trials and settlement scale-up
keyDates.detail: First Track 1 bellwether trials proceeding in 2026; global settlement matrix negotiations continue.

deadlineOverride: The Camp Lejeune Justice Act filing window closed on August 10, 2024. State filing deadlines do not control CLJA claims, and new claims are generally barred regardless of the claimant's home state. People with claims filed before the deadline do not need to refile; pending claims continue through the Navy administrative process and the Eastern District of North Carolina.
